content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from ICM/RND curious agent frameworks) can be repurposed as a prioritization gate for active learning agent memory, where only information that sparks irreducible agent curiosity is retained in long-term parametric memory, while low-curiosity predictable observations are offloaded to non-episodic external storage. This creates a self-organizing memory hierarchy that scales with the agent’s own learning progress, rather than relying on human-designed retention rules.
